sourceLabel: SEC/FINRA investor bulletins on indexed universal life; IRS retirement plan limits
notes: Default assumptions for the IUL vs 401(k) vs taxable brokerage comparison calculator (read by src/lib/iul-comparison.ts). Every number here is a broad, editable educational default — NOT a quote from any insurer or fund. TODO(owner): verify each value marked below against current insurer illustrations, fund fee tables, and IRS announcements before relying on them.

market._expectedTotalReturnPct_note: Long-run S&P 500 nominal total return has averaged roughly 9–10%/yr over very long windows. Deliberately set below 10 to avoid flattering any account type. TODO(owner): revisit annually.
market._dividendYieldPct_note: S&P 500 dividend yield has recently run ~1.2–1.4%; long-term average is higher. Matters because IUL index crediting typically uses a PRICE index that excludes dividends. TODO(owner): verify current yield.
market._badSequenceReturnsPct_note: S&P 500 total returns 2000–2011 (the 'lost decade' plus 2008), cycled across the horizon when the bad-sequence toggle is on. Source: S&P Dow Jones Indices annual total returns. TODO(owner): spot-check the series.

iul._capRatePct_note: Annual point-to-point S&P 500 cap rates on new IUL policies have commonly ranged ~8–10% in recent years, and insurers can lower caps on in-force policies. TODO(owner): verify against 2–3 current insurer illustrations.
iul._participationRatePct_note: 100% participation with a cap is the most common crediting design; some designs trade higher caps for lower participation. TODO(owner): verify.
iul._floorPct_note: 0% floor is standard — credited interest can't be negative, but policy charges still apply in down years, so cash value can still fall.
iul._premiumLoadPct_note: Premium expense charges commonly run ~5–10% of each premium. TODO(owner): verify against current illustrations.
iul._annualPolicyCostPct_note: Simplified combined annual drag (cost of insurance + admin + rider charges) expressed as % of cash value. Real COI is per-$1000-of-net-amount-at-risk, rises with age, and varies enormously by age/health/design — early-year drag is typically much higher than this flat assumption. TODO(owner): replace with values from an actual in-force illustration.
iul._faceAmountMultiple_note: Modeled initial death benefit = annual premium × this multiple (e.g. $10k/yr → $200k face). Real face amounts depend on age, health, and GPT/CVAT test design. TODO(owner): adjust to match a real illustration.
iul._corridorFactor_note: Simplified IRC §7702 corridor: death benefit shown as max(face, cash value × this factor). Real corridor factors vary by age.

k401._employerMatchPct_note: Modeled as employer adding this % on top of each $1 the user contributes. A common real-world design (50% match up to 6% of salary) lands near 25–50% of typical contributions. TODO(owner): users should set their own plan's match.
k401._annualFeePct_note: All-in 401(k) plan + fund fee. Large plans run well under 0.5%; small plans can exceed 1%. TODO(owner): verify.
k401._retirementTaxRatePct_note: Assumed ordinary income tax rate on traditional 401(k) withdrawals in retirement. TODO(owner): users should set their own expected bracket.
k401._employeeLimit2026Usd_note: 2026 employee elective deferral limit (announced by IRS Nov 2025; catch-up extra for 50+). TODO(owner): verify against the current IRS COLA notice before publishing updates.

taxable._fundFeePct_note: Broad-market index fund expense ratios commonly run 0.02–0.20%. TODO(owner): verify.
taxable._dividendTaxPct_note: Qualified dividend rate for most middle/upper-middle brackets (0/15/20% + possible 3.8% NIIT). TODO(owner): verify for target audience.
taxable._capGainsPct_note: Long-term capital gains rate for most filers (0/15/20% + possible NIIT). Note: under current law, heirs receive a stepped-up basis on taxable accounts at death — highly relevant to wealth-transfer comparisons. TODO(owner): verify.
